sections.paragraph:
δειλαίνω aor. pass. 3 sg. subj. δειλανθῇ 1 Macc 5:41 (‘act the coward’ Aristot. et al.) mid. be cowardly, fearful (Ps.-Lucian, Ocy. 153; PTebt 58, 27 [III b.c.]; PLond VI, 1914, 24; 1 Macc 5:41) IRo 5:2. μηδὲν δειλαινόμενος without fearing Hs 9, 1, 3.—DELG s.v. δείδω p. 256.

aor. aor. = aorist

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

  1. sg. = singular

subj. subj. = subjunctive; subject

Aristot Aristot , various works, IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Ps. Ps. = pseudo, pseudonymous

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

PTebt PTebt = The Tebtunis Papyri—List 4

PLond PLond = PLondon=Greek Papyri in the British Museum—List 4

IRo IRo = Ignatius to the Romans—List 1

Hs Hs = Similitudes

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 215
